review_notes: The functions are applicable to Kubernetes as they relate to vulnerability monitoring and scanning. The function names mostly follow the naming conventions but required minor adjustments for consistency and clarity. The technical mapping is accurate as image scanning and admission controllers are key components in enforcing vulnerability policies in Kubernetes.
naming_improvements: Use 'admission' instead of 'admission_controller' for resource name to maintain consistency
title: CCCS RA-5: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning
step1_reasoning: The control CCCS RA-5 focuses on vulnerability monitoring and scanning, which can be mapped to Kubernetes through image vulnerability scanning and admission control policies that enforce vulnerability checks. Kubernetes environments can implement image scanning on admission to ensure that only images without known vulnerabilities are deployed. Additionally, admission controllers can enforce policies that prevent the deployment of vulnerable images or configurations. Pod security standards can also include checks for known vulnerabilities.
